description: Characteristic MegaETH workloads for the replay-throughput benchmark. Each case is a self-contained state-test fixture (pre-state + tx + env + megaEnv), replayed offline in isolation — no RPC, no archive node. Most are real mainnet transactions dumped via `mega-evme replay --dump-fixture`; non-on-chain cases (e.g. attack snapshots) use the same format. See run.py and README.md.

cases.name: system_call
cases.note: Call into a 0x6342… system contract — exercises interceptor dispatch and volatile-data/oracle paths.

cases.name: medium_call
cases.note: Medium contract call with no logs — baseline compute + a few SLOAD/SSTORE.

cases.name: log_heavy
cases.note: 12 logs — exercises LOG opcodes and the dual (compute + storage) gas metering on topics/data.

cases.name: large_defi
cases.note: Large multi-call DeFi transaction — deep call stack, many storage writes.

cases.name: attack_deploy
cases.note: Mainnet attack contract deployment captured as a self-contained prestate fixture (not a mined tx; converted from crates/mega-evm/benches/fixtures/known_attack_deploy.json, #299). 142M gas of CREATE — the heaviest stress on the multi-dimensional AdditionalLimit accounting. Carries an empty `post`, so it names its `spec` explicitly.
